title: Simple PCR From a Selected Core Region

catalog.notes: Beginner-friendly selection-first PCR walkthrough centered on core ROI, maximum primer distance from the core, and maximum amplicon length.

generated_chapter.summary: Open a ready local TP73 sequence and walk through the smallest useful PCR story: select the core ROI, limit primer distance from the core, and cap the amplicon length.
generated_chapter.narrative: This chapter is intentionally minimal. The tutorial project opens one stable local TP73 locus so you can stay in the GUI, paint or select one region of interest, launch `Simple PCR from selection`, and reason about primer placement in beginner terms before moving on to the richer batch-PCR chapter.
use_cases: You want the shortest possible path from one selected region to one PCR design attempt.
You want to learn the meaning of core ROI, maximum primer distance from the core, and maximum amplicon length without queueing or painting multiple regions.
You want one offline tutorial project that opens directly into local TP73 context before using the PCR Designer.
gui_steps: Open `File -> Open Tutorial Project... -> Core -> 18. Simple PCR From a Selected Core Region`.
In the opened TP73 project, keep the DNA map in linear mode and drag over one short core region that must be included in the amplicon.
Right-click the selection and choose `Simple PCR from selection`.
In `PCR Designer`, adjust `max primer distance from core` and `max amplicon`, then click `Apply simple flank windows` if you changed the distance.
Run `Design Primer Pairs` and inspect the in-panel primer report preview for left/right distance from the core ROI and whether the pair cleanly flanks the core.
learning_objectives: Start a simple PCR directly from one sequence selection.
Interpret the beginner PCR controls as deterministic flank-window constraints around the selected core ROI.
Review saved primer pairs in terms of amplicon length and left/right distance from the core ROI.

parameter_notes.where_used: map selection and PCR Designer starter block
parameter_notes.why_it_matters: This is the part of the biology that must definitely be included in the PCR product.
parameter_notes.how_to_derive: Select only the indispensable sequence interval, not the full desired amplicon.

parameter_notes.why_it_matters: This sets how far upstream and downstream primers may move away from the required core region.
parameter_notes.how_to_derive: Choose the largest flank width you are comfortable allowing for primer search on each side of the core ROI.

parameter_notes.why_it_matters: This caps product length so the simple-PCR result stays experimentally practical.
parameter_notes.how_to_derive: Set the longest acceptable product length for the assay before running primer design.

follow_up_commands: cargo run --bin gentle_cli -- shell 'ui open pcr-design'
checkpoints: The tutorial project opens with local TP73 sequence `tp73_locus` already loaded.
A non-empty selection exposes `Simple PCR from selection` in the DNA-window context menu.
The PCR Designer shows the `Simple PCR starter` block.
After primer design, the report preview shows left/right distance from the core ROI and whether the pair cleanly flanks the core.
